treatment_information.treatment_response: Used for the treatment of seizures, patient was weaned off using electroencephalogram recordings
Patient improved back to her motor baseline with only mild residual speech deficits

treatment_timeline.event: Patient presented with episodes of right-sided facial twitching and increasing lethargy, progressing to right hemiparesis
Patient was intubated due to concerns for continued seizure and monitored in the paediatric intensive care unit
CT and MRI were obtained, revealing a left frontotemporal hypoattenuation and abnormal tortuous vessels
Catheter cerebral angiogram was performed, revealing a collection of vessels draining into the superior sagittal sinus via the vein of Trolard, concerning for a DVA
Patient was treated acutely with propofol infusion for seizures
Patient was started on levetiracetam, 500 mg two times a day
Patient was weaned off propofol using electroencephalogram recordings and extubated
MRI demonstrated recanalisation of the occluded DVA

patient_outcome.final_status: Improved back to her motor baseline with only mild residual speech deficits
patient_outcome.follow_up_details: 3-month follow-up MRI demonstrated recanalisation of the occluded DVA
